text: That’s kind of how xG works, but it’s very misleading. xG is basically a probability of each shot going in. Then the probabilities are summed. That xG means the average chance of one of those shots going in was less than 6% (3.45/62 =0.055). That’s abysmal chance creation. For a bit of further nuance, with a couple saves and the post strike, a few chances probably had higher probabilities, meaning outside 3 or 4 actual chances created, every other shot had negligible chances of scoring

text: Doesn't matter which ranking they achieve among the third-placed teams, only matters which other seven groups qualify alongside them. See the incredibly long list of permutations where it's effectively set out which team plays which other one, regardless of how 'well' they qualified. If a team from Group I goes through, which of the six group winners it will play just depends on who advances alongside them. Basically read the left block of columns as 'Teams from this group went through' and the right block of colums as 'These are the group winners they will play'.
